questions.text: Have safety procedures been evaluated to ensure they are compliant with OH&S regulations?
questions.judgement_criteria: The respondent can explain how safety procedures have been evaluated to ensure they are compliant with OH&S regulations.

questions.text: Does site manager review lagging OH&S KPIs (e.g. number of incidents, frequency, severity) at least monthly?
questions.judgement_criteria: Site leadership follows safety lagging OH&S KPIs, and give directions to review the safety program if necessary.

questions.text: Are the root causes of incidents categorized and then tracked?
questions.judgement_criteria: The cause of incident in the most recent year has been determined and the countermeasures are followed-up by the termination.

questions.text: Are there repercussions/consequences for teammates that do not follow the Safety Rules and Procedures?
questions.judgement_criteria: Site manager can show disciplinary procedure or documented examples (e.g. consequence matrix) of carrying out disciplinary action, etc.

questions.text: Does site manager follow and review "Leading indicators" in their safety result tracking?
questions.judgement_criteria: Site leadership follow not only lagging indicator(e.g. # of injuries), but also leading indicators, and give directions to review and/or improve safety programs if necessary.

questions.text: When creating the annual (or more) safety implementation plan, do you consider the KPI result/progress in previous year?
questions.judgement_criteria: The respondent can explain an example that was changed from annual planning for previous year and its reason.

questions.text: Do you motivate teammates to find and implement new best practices relevant to safety in other BS sites?
questions.judgement_criteria: Site manager can explain how he/she motivate teammates to find best practices and how he/she allocate resources to implement them. (e.g. Teammates join in safety meeting where other site's members are present)

questions.text: Does the site management team benchmark Safety and DP trends in logistics industry / other external companies and consider incorporation into site targets/activities?
questions.judgement_criteria: The respondent can give example of Safety and DP trends which the management team referred.

questions.text: Have you monitored the H&S and DP regulations* which may be applicable for your site and checked that these regulations have been taken place in your sites? (e.g. trainings, mobile equipment, chemicals, flammable materials)
questions.judgement_criteria: The H&S and DP regulations are listed up and actions in the site are determined. Besides, the site is compliant with these regulations.

questions.text: Are safety initiatives being implemented to improve safety? Is there any document to show that?
questions.judgement_criteria: Site leadership can show a plan to improve safety activities in the site and explain that the plan is implemented.

questions.text: Do you know how important teammates' involvement in safety is?　Besides, do you explain the importance to teammates and involve them in safety activities?
questions.judgement_criteria: Site manager could both explain why teammate involvement in safety management is important and can provide examples of actions taken to promote this and the results of those actions.

questions.text: Is there a H&S meeting with representation from teammates, leadership and site H&S (if available)? Does leadership give directions to solve issues?
questions.judgement_criteria: The meeting minute indicates that the meetings are held at planned intervals and the participants discuss the improvement of safety on site.

questions.text: Can you show examples of implemented safety measures based on teammate feedback?
questions.judgement_criteria: The respondents can show an example of ongoing safety project with allocated budgets.

questions.text: Have you gathered best practices on safety activity from GBS/SBU, and implement them in the site?
questions.judgement_criteria: The respondents can give examples of industry best practices, where they came from and how they are being implemented on site.

questions.text: Does your site manager consider safety a priority and address safety at the morning assembly, roll-call, etc.?
questions.judgement_criteria: All respondents can show examples that the site manager addresses at the morning assembly, roll call, etc.

questions.text: What does site leadership (e.g. site manager and supervisor) do when a serious incident  (lost-time-injury, fire accident, etc.) occurs?
questions.judgement_criteria: All respondents stated that site leadership (e.g. site manager and supervisor) was involved in the root cause analysis process when a serious incident (lost-time-injury, fire accident, etc.) occurred

questions.text: Does Site leaders actively provide feedback to teammates and promote safety whilst on tours?
questions.judgement_criteria: The respondents can show an example that they provide feedbacks to teammates to ensure adherence to safety rules, such as procedures, standards, etc.

questions.text: What is your responsibility for safety?
questions.judgement_criteria: All respondents can explain their own responsibilities for safety. (e.g. participation of safety activity, etc.)

questions.text: Do you recognize good safety behaviors of teammates and give them rewards?
questions.judgement_criteria: Site manager can explain an example of this kind of reward in the most recent year.

questions.text: Does management have a mid-term plan including Safety and DP items and their necessary resources to improve safety on site?
questions.judgement_criteria: The plan clarifies the Safety and/or DP items and their presumed recourses to implement.

questions.text: Is there an external/cross site safety meeting? If so, does safety manager share H&S issues or best practices?
questions.judgement_criteria: Meeting minute indicate that the respondent contributes to discussion in the safety meeting. (e.g. Site managers meeting in SBU, meeting in the local safety association, etc.)

questions.text: Do you feel comfortable stopping work if you feel there is a risk of injury?
questions.judgement_criteria: All respondents confirm that they feel safety is essential. They are comfortable stopping work when they feel their safety is compromised.

questions.text: What are H&S risks relevant to your work? What makes you recognize them?
questions.judgement_criteria: All respondents are trained to recognize the H&S risks applicable in the site.

questions.text: Are you aware of your personal impact on safety? How do you contribute to improve safety?
questions.judgement_criteria: Site manager stated that they have an impact on teammate safety. They can show an example of their contribution for safety. (e.g. They patrol on site, prioritize safety measures, allocate budget, etc.)

questions.text: Do teammates know how to report incidents?
questions.judgement_criteria: All respondents can explain how to report when incident happen. A review of the incident records from the last month show that teammates have been reporting incidents and near misses.

questions.text: What are the most important safety rules in your site/job and why?
questions.judgement_criteria: All respondents can explain their site/job safety rules and why they are important.

questions.text: Do you understand the greatest potential risks in your work, and how they are controlled?
questions.judgement_criteria: All respondents can explain the greatest potential risks in their work and controls to mitigate these risks.

questions.text: How do you encourage communication between sites to share best-practices and lessons-learned?
questions.judgement_criteria: The respondent can explain how to share H&S risks and control to mitigate these risks between sites. (e.g. Teammates introduce their safety activities to other site teammates.)

questions.text: What would be considered a priority: complete the operation plan or follow safety procedures?
questions.judgement_criteria: All respondents know that safety is considered more important than operation.

questions.text: What actions do you take during your operations to prevent reoccurrence of an incident which occurred in your site?
questions.judgement_criteria: All respondents can explain the actions required to prevent the reoccurrence of incident that occurred in their site. They stated that they are involved in the required actions.

questions.text: Are the site/job safety rules being followed?
questions.judgement_criteria: On-site observation indicates that teammates follow their site/job safety rules.

questions.text: Have teammates proposed improvements of H&S risks and risk mitigation measures at the creations or revisions of work standards, training materials, etc?
questions.judgement_criteria: Materials indicate that teammates propose improvements of H&S risks and risk mitigation measures at the creations or revisions of work standards, training materials, etc.

questions.text: What response would you expect from your colleague when you act unsafe?
questions.judgement_criteria: All respondents stated that unsafe behavior is unacceptable and anyone acting unsafe will be corrected by their peers.

questions.text: What requirement, based on safety regulations do you explain to teammate?
questions.judgement_criteria: The respondent can show an example that they explain safety requirements based on regulation to teammates.

questions.text: What incidents and the countermeasures at your site did you receive from managements recently?
questions.judgement_criteria: All respondents are informed of recent safety incidents in their site and countermeasures. Their statements match incident records.

questions.text: Are you regularly trained about your site/job safety rules?
questions.judgement_criteria: All respondents agreed that they are regularly reminded and trained of their site/job safety rules.

questions.text: Have you taken training on residual risks in your work? How are you trained?
questions.judgement_criteria: All respondents stated that they took the training on residual risks based on the work standard.

questions.text: Are there opportunities to share H&S risks and countermeasures of other sites at the safety meeting in your site?
questions.judgement_criteria: Meeting material or minute indicate that H&S risks and countermeasures of other sites have been shared at the safety meeting in your site.

questions.text: Is the legally required H&S training given to all teammates?
questions.judgement_criteria: Compliance evaluation and training records in the most recent year indicate that H&S training required by regulation has been provided to all teammates.

questions.text: Are incident investigations carried out by qualified people?
questions.judgement_criteria: A review of the last incident (injury, disease/illness, near-miss) record show that the incident was investigated by qualified people (managements or site safety), and the identified causes were added to the risk tracking.

questions.text: Are safety rule's effectiveness being evaluated by the H&S (if available), teammates and their site leaders?
questions.judgement_criteria: The site manager or site safety (if available) could describe how the H&S rule's effectiveness is evaluated, who is involved in the evaluation process and how the processes are improved after evaluation.

questions.text: Do work/job specific trainings highlight safety risks and processes associated with that work/job?
questions.judgement_criteria: Safety instructions and safety training are included in the work/job specific training materials.

questions.text: Is risk assessment taken when planning new projects/work sessions?
questions.judgement_criteria: The site manager said that risk assessment is always done in the early planning stages for upcoming projects/work sessions and could show the recent RA result .

questions.text: Do teammates communicate injuries/near-misses/defects to managers, and does management follow up them until close?
questions.judgement_criteria: All respondents can show items recently reported to him/her and the examples of follow-ups associated with the reports.

questions.text: Are H&S/DP rules communicated to teammates when updated/changed/newly created.
questions.judgement_criteria: Notifications board, document record etc show that updated/created H&S/DP rules are communicated to teammates.

questions.text: Do you know what safety initiatives are on going on site and how they are performing?
questions.judgement_criteria: All respondents could describe the most recent H&S initiatives (key projects or activities) to improve H&S management on site, and knew if the results were positive or negative.

questions.text: When a safety issue would occur, who would you report to?
questions.judgement_criteria: All respondents stated that they would report specific safety issues to their site leadership (e.g. site manager and supervisor) .

questions.text: What is a content of system and a scope for "management of change"?
questions.judgement_criteria: All supervisors could explain system and scope of management of change (4M changes).

questions.text: Do you communicate with OH&S expert about upcoming changes on site when there are any concerns on Safety?
questions.judgement_criteria: The respondent could show an example that he/she discuss the injury/illness/fire risks associated with upcoming changes to work processes with internal/external safety expert.

questions.text: What was the feedback from management on your hazard report?
questions.judgement_criteria: All respondents stated that management had taken specific actions to address hazards they reported, and these actions have been communicated to them.

questions.text: Do you receive feedback from third party on effectiveness of safety management system to imorove it?
questions.judgement_criteria: Regarding the safety management system in the sites, the respondent can explain evaluation results by third party (external, SBU Safety or Logistic Top management), feedback, and the actions.

questions.text: Does management have defined roles and responsibilities for safety activities?
questions.judgement_criteria: The site manager can explain their roles and responsibilities on safety activity in their teams.

questions.text: Is there collaboration between Logistics safety dept. and site management to address recurring incidents?
questions.judgement_criteria: A review of the last incident (injury, disease/illness, near-miss) record show evidence that site management and Logistic safety dept. collaborate to prevent reoccurrence of the accident by addressing the causation.

questions.text: Do safety training sessions for newcomers reference relevant frequent incidents and severe risks?
questions.judgement_criteria: The safety training material for newcomers includes frequent incidents, severe risks and their controls.

questions.text: Are safety processes updated and improved frequently using findings from the review?
questions.judgement_criteria: Document history of H&S procedures and standards from the last year showed that safety processes had been updated to address newly identified risks and controls based on review.

questions.text: Have you involved TMs in periodical review on safety action plans?
questions.judgement_criteria: A review of the safety action plans (ex. meeting minutes, RA, etc.) from the most recent safety projects (safety programs or safety activities) show that teammates were involved and consulted.

questions.text: Do teammates comply with safety rules and improve risks actively being conscious of safety for not only their selves, but also teammates. ?
questions.judgement_criteria: Observation in genba showed that teammates are actively conscious of their safety and the safety of others. Activities for improvement were conducted in CFT being conscious of safety for not only their selves, but also teammates.

questions.text: Do you engage with *external parties to obtain and exchange　knowledge on safety?
questions.judgement_criteria: The site manager could explain an example that they voluntarily discussed safety with managers from other sites or external parties to improve safety knowledge.

questions.text: Have teammates been trained on the safety mission statement and high risk activity?
questions.judgement_criteria: Training records show all teammates on site have been given training on the Safety Mission Statement. Training records show that required employees have received training for high risk activities.

questions.text: Are you aware of the regulations concerning safety training?
questions.judgement_criteria: The reapondent can explain the regulations concerning safety training.

questions.text: Have role specific trainings been identified and are given to the necessary people?
questions.judgement_criteria: There are knowledge/skill matrix table for each occupation/role, necessary training is described, and the implementation or plan of training can be confirmed.

questions.text: Are the needs and the content of trainings reviewed and improved after an accident occurs?
questions.judgement_criteria: Training documents indicate that training was reviewed after an accident.

questions.text: Are trainings reviewed and adjusted when safety rules and controls that involve that specific subject matter change?
questions.judgement_criteria: Revision history of treaining documents indicate that training have been adjusted based on relevant safety control changes.

questions.text: When new hazards are identified, are training material revised and communicated to teammates?
questions.judgement_criteria: Training material and training records indicate that new identified risks are added to the training material and communicated to teammates.

questions.text: Are trainings updated with industry best practices?
questions.judgement_criteria: The respondent can show an example of trainings being updated with industry best practices.

questions.text: Is the effectiveness of a training reviewed based on pre and post training assessments? Is the training improved based on the assessment result?
questions.judgement_criteria: There is evidence of pre and post training assessments. The respondent can show an example of the improvement based on the assessment results.

questions.text: Are the roles & responsibilities of emergency organization defined in the emergency response policy/procedure that complies with legal & local regulations?
questions.judgement_criteria: The site emergency response policy/procedure has clarified the roles & responsibilities of each emerfency responders and been checked by the safety team to ensure the compliance with legal & local regulation.

questions.text: Do emergency drills periodically take place?
questions.judgement_criteria: There is evidence of an emergency drill performed within the last 12 months.

questions.text: Are the emergency procedures reviewed and updated based on the results of drills or actual emergencies?
questions.judgement_criteria: The emergency response procedure was updated based on the review on the drills or performance of actual emergencies.

questions.text: Do emergency response plans which have clear rules and guidelines address each potential scenarios?
questions.judgement_criteria: Multiple potential scenarios are identified and each responses have been defined with clear rules and guidelines.

questions.text: Have the drills been conducted by every shifts and cover each identified potential scenarios?
questions.judgement_criteria: Drills on every identified potential scenarios have been performed in each shifts.

questions.text: Are best practices in other sites incorporated into the procedure for emergency?
questions.judgement_criteria: The respondent can show an example that the best practices in other sites are incorporated into the site procedure for emergency. or There is a evidence of the comparison with other sites.

questions.text: When implementation of regular OH&S communication and OH&S training, does the site manager use the language where the teammates are familiar?
questions.judgement_criteria: There is no difference between the language used by teammates and the language used in the sampled communication record or training material. (or Multiple forms are used and language differences are accounted for.)

questions.text: Are the causes of accidents/incidents communicated to teammates?
questions.judgement_criteria: The respondent indicates that the causes of incidents are communicated to him/her and how these information are communicated.

questions.text: Are site safety rules communicated to workers in a clear and structured manner?
questions.judgement_criteria: The sampled teammates all say that they are well informed and can explain how the information communicated contributes to their safety.

questions.text: Does management inform workers of newly found hazards and risks in a timely manner?
questions.judgement_criteria: The respondent is able to provide at least one recent example of top-down communication on hazards and risks.

questions.text: Do teammates communicate hazards and risks to their supervisors as soon as they identify them?
questions.judgement_criteria: The respondent is able to provide at least one recent example of bottom-up communication on hazards and risks.

questions.text: Do you communicate improvements with your TMs to evaluate the effectiveness against unsafe conditions or near misses that TMs have reported?
questions.judgement_criteria: The respondent can show an example of record that improvements against unsafe conditions or near misses TM reported are communicated.

questions.text: Do you conduct the LOTO training to the teammates who perform servicing or maintenance tasks, and distribute the Lock-out & Tag-out devices?
questions.judgement_criteria: Training record indicates that the teammates who perform servicing or maintenance tasks have been trained on LOTO. On-site observation indicate that service and maintenance teammates who need LOTO have locks and tags. LOTO

questions.text: Is the LOTO standard reviewed after incidents, audits or opportunity areas have been identified?
questions.judgement_criteria: The respondent can show an example that LOTO standard (site standard, ECP, work standard, etc.) is reviewed in response to incidents, audit findings, authority guidance and concerns raised by teammates. The respondence agrees with the revision history of LOTO standard.

questions.text: Is the LOTO system being used and applied by teammates performing maintenance tasks?
questions.judgement_criteria: LOTO standard (site standard, ECP, work standard, etc.) was followed by all teammates observed performing maintenance/operation tasks that required LOTO. In case that LOTO operation cannot be observed on site, confirm site tour records, etc. LOTO

questions.text: Is priodical monitoring on LOTO activity performed to verify that LOTO are properly implemented when required?
questions.judgement_criteria: The respondent can show any sampled evidence where check results are filled out. LOTO

questions.text: Are ECPs (Energy Control Procedures) prepared for all applicable machinery? Do they include all applicable energy sources (electrical, mechanical, pneumatic, etc.)?
questions.judgement_criteria: ECP includes all applicable energy source (electrical, mechanical, pneumatic, etc.) has been prepared for all applicable machinery. LOTO

questions.text: Have you gathered best practices on LOTO from other sites/industry, and considered to incorporate them into the site procedure?
questions.judgement_criteria: The respondent can show an example that the best practices on LOTO have been gathered from other sites, and it has been incorporated into the site procedure. or There is a evidence of the comparison with other sites.

questions.text: Do tyou know the safety rules that are required to be followed? 　(8 common safety rules, mobile equipment rules, crane & hoist rules and tire inflation rules applying to his/her job position)
questions.judgement_criteria: All respondents know the applicable All Bridgestone Safety Rules in their job positions.

questions.text: Is the effectiveness of the Site Safety Rules reviewed after incidents, audits or opportunity areas have been identified?
questions.judgement_criteria: The respondent can show an example that Site Safety Rules are reviewed and improved.

questions.text: Are the applicable BS common safety rules followed by the teammates?
questions.judgement_criteria: All respondents can demonstrate knowledge of the Safety Rules and explain key points on safety in the work standard.

questions.text: Have you ever engaged to manage and evaluate the risks involved in their work?
questions.judgement_criteria: Therespondent can show an example that they collaboratd with management to evaluate risks in the work area.

questions.text: Are safety rules developed/adjusted after these evaluations take place?
questions.judgement_criteria: The respondent can give an example that safety rules were updated after risk evaluation with teammates.

questions.text: Is feedback from teammates considered to improve safety rules?
questions.judgement_criteria: The respondent can show an example that thier feedback was used to revise the Safety Rules. (Examples of FB: Teammates require to change safety rule due to difficulties of the compliance with the required safety rules during tasks/services, etc.)

questions.text: Have you gathered best practices on safety rules(e.g. work standards) from other sites/industry, and considered to incorporate them into the site procedure?
questions.judgement_criteria: The respondent can show an example that the respondent can show an example that the best practices on Safety rules have been gathered from other sites/industry, and it has been incorporated into the site procedure. Or There is a evidence of the comparison with other sites.

questions.text: Have you communicated Work at height requirements to teammates performing Work at height?
questions.judgement_criteria: There is the training and/or communication record for teammates performing Work at height.

questions.text: What are the countermeasures against a incident involving work at height which occurred in your site in last 1 year?
questions.judgement_criteria: All respondents can explain sampled countermeasures against the incident involving work at height which occurred in the site.

questions.text: Are Work at height controls being used and applied by teammates performing tasks that require Work at height?
questions.judgement_criteria: On-site observaion indicate that all observed teammates show good application of Work at height safety rules. In case that Work at height operation cannot be observed on site, confirm site tour records, etc.

questions.text: Are Work at height controls reviewed and improved after new risks are identified?
questions.judgement_criteria: The respondent can explain the recent identified risks and their follow-up actions.

questions.text: Have you gathered best practices on safety rules(e.g. work standards) from other sites/industry, and considered to incorporate them into the site procedure?
questions.judgement_criteria: The respondent can show an example that the best practices on Work at height have been gathered from other sites/industry, and it has been incorporated into the site procedure. or There is a evidence of the comparison with other plants.

questions.text: Have you communicated requirements on mobile equipment to the drivers?
questions.judgement_criteria: There is the training and/or communication record for mobile equipment drivers.

questions.text: What are the countermeasures against a incident involving mobile equipment which occurred in your site in last 1 year?
questions.judgement_criteria: All respondents can explain sampled countermeasures against the incident involving mobile equipment which occurred in the site.

questions.text: Do all drivers follow the rules and procedures for mobile equipment?
questions.judgement_criteria: On-site observaion indicate that all observed teammates show good application of mobile equipment safety rules. In case that forklift operation cannot be observed on site, confirm site tour records, etc.

questions.text: Are Mobile Equipment controls reviewed and iproved after new risks are identified?
questions.judgement_criteria: The respondent can explain the recent identified risks and their follow-up actions.

questions.text: Is feedback from drivers considered when adapting the mobile equipment standard?
questions.judgement_criteria: The respondent can show an example that thier feedback is used to revise the rule or procedure for mobile equipment. (Examples of FB: Teammates require changes due to difficulties of the compliance with the safety standards for mobile equipment, etc.)

questions.text: Have you gathered best practices on mobile equipment from other sites/industry, and considered to incorporate them into the site procedure?
questions.judgement_criteria: The respondent can show an example that the best practices on mobile equipment have been gathered from other sites/industry, and it has been incorporated into the site procedure. or There is a evidence of the comparison with other plants.

questions.text: Has the need for 3S(Select & remove, Sort, Keep clean) in the work area been communicated?
questions.judgement_criteria: There is evidence that the need of 3S has been communicated to teammates. 3S

questions.text: Are 3S visual standards displayed in work area?
questions.judgement_criteria: 3S Documents are displayed in work areas. 3S

questions.text: Have 3S leaders been assigned from shop floor workers and have 3S self check by 3S leaders been periodically performed?
questions.judgement_criteria: 3S leaders has been assigned and there is evidence of 3S self check performed by the 3S leaders. 3S

questions.text: Are all unnecessary objects being removed or planned to be removed from the work area ?
questions.judgement_criteria: All inspected work areas were cleaned (no debris on floor, no spillages) as defined in the 3S visual standard, and there were no unnecessary objects or plans to remove unnecessary objects.

questions.text: Are necessary equipment/tools being put in clearly marked and fixed positions?
questions.judgement_criteria: All necessary items are stored in their clearly marked space/position.

questions.text: Do teammates proceed with 3S activity for the improvement of 3S level?
questions.judgement_criteria: On-site observation indicated that 3S activity for the improvement of 3S level has been proceeded. or 3S documents indicated that improvement of 3S level can be confirmed or 3S level is over 4.

questions.text: Have you gathered best practices on 3S from other sites/industry, and considered to incorporate them into the site procedure?
questions.judgement_criteria: The respondent can show an example that the best practices on 3S have been gathered from other sites/industry, and it has been incorporated into the site procedure. or There is a evidence of the comparison with other sites.

questions.text: How do you identify and ensure compliance to crane and hoist relevant safety legislation ?
questions.judgement_criteria: The respondent can explain the regulations and give at least one method for ensuring compliance.

questions.text: Is there an established crane and hoist safety standard that complies with legal requirements?
questions.judgement_criteria: There is a crane and hoist safety standard that highlights the legal requirements.

questions.text: Is there a list of licensed crane operators?
questions.judgement_criteria: There is an official list of licensed crane operators that highlights the expiry dates of their licenses.

questions.text: What are the countermeasures against a hoist & crane incident which occurred in your site in last 1 year?
questions.judgement_criteria: All respondents can explain sampled countermeasures against hoist & crane incident which occurred in the site.

questions.text: Do crane operators follow the rules and standards concerning cranes and hoist operations?
questions.judgement_criteria: All crane operators work according to the crane and hoist safety rules stated in the crane and hoist procedure (Operator and others were kept away from hoisted loads, there was a work leader that gave hand signals, hoisted load's stability was checked with a lift of a few inches, etc.). In case that hoist and crane operation cannot be observed on site, confirm site tour records, etc.

questions.text: Do crane operators provide feedback (safety perspective) to their peers inside and outside of their site?
questions.judgement_criteria: Crane operators observe each other and provide feedback. Or Information on how to operate crane safely has been shared.

questions.text: Have you gathered best practices on　crane and hoist from other sites/external companies, and considered to incorporate them into the site procedure?
questions.judgement_criteria: The respondent can show an example that the best practices on crane and hoist have been gathered from other sites/industry, and it has been incorporated into the site procedure. or There is a evidence of the comparison with other sites.

questions.text: Have the the process and the importance of KY been communicated to the teammates?
questions.judgement_criteria: There is evidence of a defined KY process that been communicated with teammates.

questions.text: Have a KY leader and KY representatives been appointed?
questions.judgement_criteria: There are an officially appointed KY leader and KY representatives on site.

questions.text: Does everyone on site participate in KYT?
questions.judgement_criteria: KYT records indicate that all teammates and temporary staff have participated in KYT within the last 3 months. KYT

questions.text: Are the results from KY used to develop new safety rules or adjust old rules or improvement of safety measures for hazards((risk mitigation/administrative measures)?
questions.judgement_criteria: The respondent can provide evidence of a improvement of safety measures for hazards or a safety rule that was recently changed because of the KY system's recommendations.

questions.text: Is KYT conducted periodically in accordance with SBU or/and site standard by everyone on site?
questions.judgement_criteria: KYT records indicate that KYT is completed periodically in accordance with SBU and/or site standard by everyone on site. KYT

questions.text: Is the KYT activity evaluated and opportunity for improvement discussed with the site manager annually?
questions.judgement_criteria: The respondent can explain the evaluation result of KYT activity and improvement discussed with the site manager last time.

questions.text: Have you gathered best practices and/or risks found in other sites during KY, and considered to incorporate them into site procedure?
questions.judgement_criteria: The respondent can show an example that the best practices and/or risks found in other sites during KY have been gathered, and it has been incorporated into the site procedure. or There is a evidence of the comparison with other ites.

questions.text: Has the tire inflation standard been communicated to the teammates?
questions.judgement_criteria: The respondent can explain the key points which need to pay attention to in the tire inflation process.

questions.text: Is there a clearly marked area specifically for tire inflation?
questions.judgement_criteria: There is a clearly marked tire inflation area.

questions.text: Is basic safety equipment available in the inflation area?
questions.judgement_criteria: All the required safety equipment as stated in the Tire Inflation Operation procedure is in the inflation area.

questions.text: Do workers in the tire inflation area follow the safety rules concerning tire inflation?
questions.judgement_criteria: All observed operators work in accordance to the Tire Inflation procedure's safety rules. In case that tire inflation operation cannot be observed on site, confirm site tour records, etc.

questions.text: Do the workers know how to effectively mitigate the risks in the tire inflation process?
questions.judgement_criteria: Sampled teammates can all give some examples of how the H&S risks in the tire inflation area are mitigated.

questions.text: Have you gathered best practices on tire inflation from other sites/industry, and considered to incorporate them into the site procedure?
questions.judgement_criteria: The respondent can show an example that the best practices on tire inflation have been gathered from other sites/industry, and it has been incorporated into the site procedure. or There is a evidence of the comparison with other sites.

questions.text: Do you certify if contractor employees who perform unload/load the tires have required skills? (e.g. mobile equipment)
questions.judgement_criteria: There is evidence to certify that contractor employees who perform unload/load the tires have required skills. (e.g. mobile equipment) BS

questions.text: Are pre-work meetings conducted prior to the start of work, are risks identified and risk mitigation measures/administrative controls defined.
questions.judgement_criteria: There is evidence that pre-work meetings are conducted prior to the start of work, risks are identified and risk mitigation measures/administrative controls are defined.

questions.text: Do all contractor employees receive contractor H&S orientation/training prior to the start of the work?
questions.judgement_criteria: There are records that all contractors received H&S orientation/training from BS prior to the work. BS

questions.text: Does the material of contractor H&S orientation/training includes emergency procedures, introduction to the Safety Mission Statement and Safety fundamental Activities?
questions.judgement_criteria: The material of contractor H&S orientation/training includes emergency procedures, introduction to the Safety Mission Statement and Safety fundamental Activities.

questions.text: Does BS perform safety patrols of contractor's work area, and confirm that risk mitigation measures/administrative controls defined at the pre-work meeting are conducted?
questions.judgement_criteria: There is evidence that BS teammates perform safety patrols of contractor's work area, and confirm that risk mitigation measures/administrative controls defined at the pre-work meeting are conducted. BS

questions.text: Have you gathered best practices on contractor management from other sites/external companies, and considered to incorporate them into the site procedure? (E.g. Daily JSA, using the external consultant for selecting contractors, etc.)
questions.judgement_criteria: The respondent can show an example that the best practices on contractor management have been gathered from other plants/industry, and it has been incorporated into the site procedure. or There is a evidence of the comparison with other sites.

questions.text: Are large tire manual handling operations identified and justified? Is there a procedure for each of these operations?
questions.judgement_criteria: Operations requiring manual handling of large tires are properly identified and justified. A working procedure exists for each.

questions.text: Has the Large Tire Manual Handling Operation procedure been communicated to the relevant people?
questions.judgement_criteria: Training records show that all relevant people within the site have been given training on the Large Tire Manual Handling Operations procedure.

questions.text: Is the large tire manual handling operation procedure reviewed in response to incidents?
questions.judgement_criteria: The revision history of the large tire manual handling operations procedure indicate that it was reviewed based on a reported incident

questions.text: Is the procedure followed accordingly by teammates when manually handling large tires?
questions.judgement_criteria: All observed teammates who execute large tire manual handling comply with its procedure. In case the operation cannot be observed on site during SMA, confirm site tour records, etc.

questions.text: Is the procedure reviewed when new activities are introduced on the site or when teammates offer relevant feedback?
questions.judgement_criteria: The revision history of the large tire manual handling operations procedure indicate that it was reviewed based on new activities introduced or teammates feedback

questions.text: Are teammates re-trained in the large tire manual handling operations procedure when required (e.g. returning from absence, changes in procedure)
questions.judgement_criteria: Training records show that teammates are re-trained in the large tire manual handling operations procedure when required (e.g. returning from absence, changes in procedure)

questions.text: Are best industry practices and new advances in large tire manual handling equipment considered to modify or improve procedures on site?
questions.judgement_criteria: The respondent can show an example that the best practices on Large Tire Manual Handling Operations have been gathered from industry and have been incorporated into the site procedure. or There is a evidence of information exchange and the comparison with other sites.

questions.text: Have the OH&S and Fire risks in the site evaluated?
questions.judgement_criteria: There is evidence that OH&S and/or Fire risks in the site are evaluated.

questions.text: Does the risk evaluation address the foreseeable hazards related to machine?
questions.judgement_criteria: There is evidence that machines risks in the site are evaluated.

questions.text: Do you have implementation plan for risk control measures against risk category III and IV?
questions.judgement_criteria: There is the implementation plan of risk control on risk category III & IV.

questions.text: Does the risk evaluation address the foreseeable hazards related to chemical and ergonomics?
questions.judgement_criteria: There is evidence that checmical & ergonomics risks in the site are evaluated.

questions.text: Are all risks of category III and IV in the risk register covered by administrative or technical controls?
questions.judgement_criteria: A review of sampled risk assessment record shows that the majority of risks are covered by administrative and engineering controls.

questions.text: Have risk assessment periodically reviewed to verify that hazards are definitely identified and risks are properly evaluated?
questions.judgement_criteria: The respondent can show an example of risk assessment documents which are periodically updated.

questions.text: Are best industry practices or new technology considered to mitigate the OH&S and fire risks on site?
questions.judgement_criteria: The respondent can show an example that the best practices or new technology have been gathered from industry and have been incorporated to mitigate the OH&S and Fire risks on site. Or there is a evidence of information exchange and the comparison with other sites.

questions.text: To whom do you report when incidents are identified?
questions.judgement_criteria: The respondents can explain the person to whom they have to report.

questions.text: Are the incidents that occurred at other sites (BS group or external) communicated?
questions.judgement_criteria: The respondents can show an example of incident that occurred at other site

questions.text: Are countermeasures determined when incidents* occurred?
questions.judgement_criteria: There is evidence of an incident report with determined countermeasures.

questions.text: Are investigation conducted when you receive SBU recurrence prevention request ?
questions.judgement_criteria: There is evidence of investigation results on site based on reurrence prevention request

questions.text: Is root cause analysis implemented when incidents* occurred?
questions.judgement_criteria: There is evidence of an incident report with root cause analysis. JS

questions.text: Have countermeasures been implemented for incidents*?
questions.judgement_criteria: Countermeasures are being implemented on site following incidents reports

questions.text: Do you have implementation plan to take countermeasures for identified risks after investigation based on recurrence prevention request?
questions.judgement_criteria: There is evidenve of implementation plan to take countermeasures for similar incident preventions.

questions.text: Are risk control measurements being executed to similar tasks, machines or processes following a reported incident*?
questions.judgement_criteria: The respondent can show an evidence that periodical review is conducted to verify effectiveness of the implemented countermasures.

questions.text: Have countermeasures been implemented based on implementation plan for recurrence prevention?
questions.judgement_criteria: Countermeasures are being implemented on site following recurrence prevention requests

questions.text: Are industry best practices being considered when determining the countermeasures against incidents*?
questions.judgement_criteria: The respondent can show an example that the best practices have been gathered from other sites/industry, and it has been incorporated into the contermeasures. or There is a evidence of the comparison with other sites.
